verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation — it is an inner-leaflet-anchored signaling kinase with no transmembrane domain and no extracellular domain accessible from outside the cell under baseline conditions. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document a cancer-cell ecto-SRC pool on the outer leaflet of tumor cells, consistent with the well-established phenomenon of cancer ecto-kinases. Activated SRC has been detected on the extracellular face of cancer cells by surface proteomics and functional antibody studies. This qualifies as cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation). Additionally, during immunogenic cell death and apoptosis, inner-leaflet-anchored signaling proteins including SRC-family kinases can be exposed on the outer leaflet. These documented non-baseline ecto-pools prevent a clean 'no' verdict under the pre-no checklist.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Baseline localization is definitively inner-leaflet (myristoylated/palmitoylated cytoplasmic kinase); the cancer ecto-SRC pool evidence is real but the mechanism and robustness of outer-leaflet display is not as extensively characterized as classical ecto-chaperones like HSP90.
